name: Umar ibn Jundab
title: Companion of Hadramaut
shortDescription: A Shia and brave man from Kufa who joined Imam Hussain's (AS) army and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Umar ibn Jundab al-Hadrami was a brave Shia man from Kufa. He had a deep, heartfelt devotion to the Prophet's (PBUH) family.

Before the battle began on Ashura (or the night before), Umar managed to navigate through the massive enemy forces and reach the small, radiant caravan of the Master of Martyrs (AS).

On the morning of Ashura, with the start of Yazid's army's general attack (known as the first attack) where Kufan archers showered the Imam's army with arrows, he stood bravely. In this attack, Umar ibn Jundab, along with a large group of the Imam's early companions, attained the great grace of martyrdom.

By sacrificing his blood, Umar ibn Jundab proved his loyalty, and his name was forever recorded in the history of Ashura.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ جُنْدَبٍ الحَضْرَمِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya:) Peace be upon Umar ibn Jundab al-Hadrami...
